label: Serious / interest-driven buyer
description: Engaged, responsive buyer asking genuine questions, stating a real budget or use, ready to move to purchase. The highest-conversion intent — don't over-negotiate.
observable_signals: asks specific questions (measurements, condition, ship time); states a real budget or 'is this for me/a gift'; responsive and timely replies; fair or near-list offer; explains why they want it
likely_underlying_interest: Wants this specific item at a fair price and to buy with confidence (security + economic well-being).
risk_flags: over-negotiating a ready yes and souring it; missing the close by stalling on more questions

label: Lowballer
description: Opens with an offer far below ask, often with little engagement; may be a bargain-hunter, a competer, or a value-claimer testing how far you'll drop.
observable_signals: offers <50-60% of ask, often instantly; '$X take it or leave it' with no reasoning; no item-specific questions; frames it as winning / 'you're overpriced'; repeats the same low number
likely_underlying_interest: Could be a hard budget cap, habitual haggling for bragging rights, or a value-claimer probing your floor — decode before reacting.
recommended_strategy: PRIN-002; PRIN-004; PRIN-003; PRIN-009; PRIN-007; PRIN-008
risk_flags: caving to the anchor and signalling lots of room; splitting the difference reflexively; over-investing effort on a non-serious bargain-hunter

label: Anchor-shopper / comparison buyer
description: Justifies a low price by citing a cheaper alternative ('I've seen it for $25', 'another seller is cheaper'); may be a real comparison or a bluff.
observable_signals: 'I can get this cheaper elsewhere / on another account'; cites a competitor price or sold listing; 'this brand isn't worth that much'; asks you to match a price
likely_underlying_interest: Wants reassurance they're not overpaying; may be over-estimating their alternative (the cheaper one may be worse condition/unauthenticated/slow).
risk_flags: dropping price to a fabricated or non-comparable comp; disparaging the competitor instead of differentiating; matching a bluff

label: Hesitant / uncertain buyer
description: Wants the item but isn't sure — about fit, condition, authenticity, or budget; a nervous or first-time secondhand buyer. The interest is usually NOT price.
observable_signals: 'hmm, not sure', 'idk'; 'never bought secondhand before'; asks lots of basic/reassurance questions; '$40 feels like a lot for something used'; saves/likes but hesitates to commit
likely_underlying_interest: Value-doubt or fit/condition fear, not price — needs proof and confidence, not a discount.
recommended_strategy: PRIN-013; PRIN-004; PRIN-029; PRIN-030; PRIN-036; PRIN-032
risk_flags: dropping price when proof would have closed it; pressuring into reactance; skipping the small-yes ladder

label: Ghoster / staller / fence-sitter
description: Went quiet after engaging, or keeps saying 'maybe later / next payday' without committing; saved or liked but won't message or pay.
observable_signals: silence 24h+ after a question or offer; 'thinking about it', 'maybe next payday'; multiple likes/saves then no message; slow, non-committal replies; committed verbally then went silent before paying
likely_underlying_interest: An unmet blocker (price, fit, trust, timing) or simple inertia; may be conflict-averse (avoider) who flees pressure.
recommended_strategy: PRIN-015; PRIN-004; PRIN-023; PRIN-011; PRIN-033; PRIN-025
risk_flags: nagging into reactance; fabricating scarcity to force a move; no commitment device so the verbal yes evaporates
source_books: NSD; GTY; GPN; BFA; 3DN; INF; DC

label: Bundler / collaborator
description: Open and exploratory, interested in multiple items, asks 'can we make this work?' — the highest-value persona for integrative deals.
observable_signals: interested in / has liked multiple items; 'any deal if I take three?'; 'is there a way we can work something out?'; receptive to bundles, open about needs
likely_underlying_interest: Wants overall value and a fair package; happy to grow the deal if it benefits both.
risk_flags: giving away margin by stacking free concessions; complicating a buyer who actually wants just one item

label: Rude / aggressive buyer
description: Uses insults, mockery, or hostility ('is this a joke', 'total ripoff'); may be a competer being blunt, or an attacker trying to throw you off balance.
observable_signals: insults / mocking ('is this a joke', 'overpriced garbage'); ALL CAPS, derisive lowball; attacks on the item or your credibility; hostile tone with a demand
likely_underlying_interest: Often fear (of being ripped off) or a tactic to rattle you into a concession; the power comes from your reaction.
risk_flags: counter-punching (tanks the deal and your liking); caving to stop the discomfort; escalating an ambiguous-terse message you misread
source_books: NSD; GTY; GPN; BFA; INF; DC

label: Bargaining bully / extortionist
description: Treats everything as principle, uses threats (bad reviews, disputes) and serial nibbling as tactics, never reciprocates. A persona, not a style — respond on leverage, route real threats to safety.
observable_signals: 'discount or I leave a one-star'; feedback/review extortion ('pay me $50 extra or...'); repeated escalation when pushed back on; serial nibbling after a deal; 'or else' ultimatums
likely_underlying_interest: To grind you down and extract more than fair; respects only strength.
risk_flags: rewarding the threat with a concession (invites more); matching abuse / retaliating; not documenting the thread before reporting

label: Scam / off-platform actor
description: Pushes to leave the platform or sets up a payment scam — off-platform pay, overpayment/refund, fake payment screenshot, ship-before-pay, chargeback bait. SAFETY STOP, not a negotiation.
observable_signals: 'pay outside the app / Venmo F&F / this link'; 'ship it first and I'll pay you'; overpayment then 'refund me the difference'; sends a 'payment confirmation' screenshot before you see funds; asks for email/phone to 'finish off-platform'; urgency to move off-platform fast
likely_underlying_interest: To remove buyer/seller protections so they can take the item or money — not a genuine buyer.
risk_flags: HARD STOP: never negotiate, never apply balcony/bridge logic; never ship before confirmed on-platform payment; report and disengage if pressed
source_books: GTY; GPN; BFA; 3DN; INF; DC; NSD

label: 'Is it available?' low-signal pinger
description: Sends a one-line availability check with no other engagement. A weak buying signal — convert gently, don't over-negotiate.
observable_signals: 'is this still available?' and nothing else; 'still got this?'; no number, no questions, no context
likely_underlying_interest: Mild interest / casual browsing; possibly a buying signal that needs a nudge to become real.
risk_flags: a dead 'yes' that kills momentum; launching a hard pitch off a one-liner

label: Relationship / loyal / repeat buyer
description: Warm, polite, complimentary; a repeat buyer, follower, or local who values your service and the item's story. Accommodate to keep the relationship.
observable_signals: 'I bought from you before'; follows your shop / is local; warm, complimentary tone ('I love this!', 'totally fine if not'); cares about the item's story or your service; defers to you
likely_underlying_interest: Ongoing relationship and feeling valued; a small genuine concession buys big goodwill and repeat business.
risk_flags: over-charging and losing a repeat customer; treating warmth as weakness; letting an accommodator over-defer into a regretted sale

label: Skeptic / authenticity-wary buyer
description: Fixated on legitimacy and risk ('is this legit?', 'how do I know it's real?', 'don't want to get scammed'); aggression here usually springs from fear.
observable_signals: 'is this real / legit?'; 'don't want to get scammed'; over-questioning authenticity and condition; asks about your sales history / reviews
likely_underlying_interest: Security — fear of buying a fake or being defrauded; wants proof and a safety net.
risk_flags: bluffing expertise/authentication you lack; getting defensive instead of providing proof; reading their fear as an attack

label: Complainer / post-sale dispute
description: Already bought and something went wrong — not as described, damaged, late, wrong item. Acknowledge feelings, own real fault, resolve with options. Distinct from pre-sale haggling.
observable_signals: 'this isn't the color in the photos'; 'arrived damaged / not as described'; 'these don't fit at all'; 'I want a refund'; loaded blame ('this is your fault', 'you didn't mention X')
likely_underlying_interest: To feel heard and made whole; behind the anger is often fear of losing money or being dismissed.
risk_flags: jumping to a fix before acknowledging the feeling; defending good intentions before owning the harm; over-conceding (panic refund) or stonewalling on policy

label: Ambiguous-terse message (unreadable tone)
description: Two-word or cryptic message ('k.', 'wow.', 'seriously?') with no context — tone unreadable in text; do not assume hostility.
observable_signals: two-word / cryptic replies; 'wow.', 'unbelievable.', 'seriously?'; no context, no question; all-caps or no punctuation that could read several ways
likely_underlying_interest: Unknown — could be anger, sarcasm, excitement, or nothing about you; needs a neutral clarifier.
risk_flags: over-reacting to a misread negative tone; firing an irreversible defensive reply
